volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Two Bacchises (Section 1.2.145b - Section 2.2.220)

sections.label: Section 1.2.145b
sections.original: Cum videbis , tum scies .
sections.translation: When you see , then you ’ ll know .

sections.label: Section 1.2.146
sections.original: Immo neque habebis neque sinam . i prorsum domum .
sections.translation: Aye , but you shall not have one , and I won ’ t allow it . Go back again home .

sections.label: Section 1.2.147
sections.original: Omitte , Lyde , ac cave malo .
sections.translation: Do leave me alone , Lydus , and beware of mischief [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.2.147b
sections.original: Quid ? cave malo ?
sections.translation: What ? Beware of mischief ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.148
sections.original: Iam excessit mi aetas ex magisterio tuo .
sections.translation: My years are now advanced beyond your tutorship .

sections.label: Section 1.2.149
sections.original: O barathrum , ubi nunc es ? ut ego te usurpem lubens .
sections.translation: O yawning gulf , where art thou now ? How gladly would I avail myself of thee !

sections.label: Section 1.2.150
sections.original: video nimio iam multo plus quam volueram .
sections.translation: Already have I lived far longer than I could have wished .

sections.label: Section 1.2.151
sections.original: vixisse nimio satiust iam quam vivere .
sections.translation: ’ Twere much better now to have once existed than to be living still .

sections.label: Section 1.2.152
sections.original: magistron quemquam discipulum minitarier ?
sections.translation: That any pupil should thus threaten his tutor !

sections.label: Section 1.2.153
sections.original: nil moror discipulos mihi iam plenos sanguinis :
sections.translation: I want no pupils for me with heated blood [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.2.154
sections.original: valens afflictat me vacivom virium .
sections.translation: An up-grown one may harass me thus devoid of strength .

sections.label: Section 1.2.155
sections.original: Fiam , ut ego opinor , Hercules , tu autem Linus .
sections.translation: As I guess , I shall become a Hercules , and you a Linus [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.2.156
sections.original: Pol metuo magis , ne Phoenix tuis factis fuam
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I fear more that through your goings-on I shall become a Phœnix [*] , and have to tell the news to your father that you are dead .

sections.label: Section 1.2.158
sections.original: Satis historiarumst .
sections.translation: Enough of these stories .

sections.label: Section 1.2.158b
sections.original: Hic vereri perdidit .
sections.translation: This youth is lost to shame [*] ;

sections.label: Section 1.2.159
sections.original: compendium edepol haud aetati optabile
sections.translation: By my troth , you made an exchange not desirable for that age of yours ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.160
sections.original: fecisti , cum istanc inpudentiam .
sections.translation: when you gained these impudent ways .

sections.label: Section 1.2.161
sections.original: occisus hic homo est . ecquid in mentem est tibi
sections.translation: the man ’ s ruined . And does it then recur to you that you have a father ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.162b
sections.original: Tibi ego an tu mihi servos es ?
sections.translation: Am I your servant , or you mine ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.163
sections.original: Peior magister te istaec docuit , non ego .
sections.translation: Some bad master has been teaching you all this , not I . You are a scholar far more apt at these pursuits

sections.label: Section 1.2.165
sections.original: quam ad illa quae te docui , ubi operam perdidi .
sections.translation: than at those lessons which I taught you when I was losing my labour . Troth , ’ twas a bad piece of deceit you were guilty of at your age , when you concealed these vicious tendencies from myself and from your father .

sections.label: Section 1.2.168
sections.original: Istactenus tibi , Lyde , libertas datast
sections.translation: Lydus , you have thus far had liberty of speech ; that is enough . So now do you follow this way , and hold your tongue [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.1.170
sections.original: Erilis patria , salve , quam ego biennio ,
sections.translation: All hail , land of my master ! which I joyfully behold after the two years that I have been absent hence at Ephesus . I salute thee , neighbour Apollo [*] , who dost have thy shrine close by our house , and to thee do I make my prayer , that thou wilt not let me meet our old gentleman , Nicobulus , before I have seen

sections.label: Section 2.1.175
sections.original: prius cónvenire quam sodalem viderim
sections.translation: Pistoclerus , the friend of Mnesilochus , him to whom Mnesilochus has sent the letter about his mistress , Bacchis .

sections.label: Section 2.2.178
sections.original: Mirumst me ut redeam te opere tanto quaesere ,
sections.translation: ’ Tis strange that you are so earnestly begging me to return , who am able by no possible means to depart hence , if I were willing ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.180
sections.original: ita me vadatum amore vinctumque adtines .
sections.translation: so bound down [*] , and so enchained with love do you hold me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.181
sections.original: Pro di immortales , Pistoclerum conspicor .
sections.translation: O , ye immortal Gods , I espy Pistoclerus ! O , Pistoclerus , hail !

sections.label: Section 2.2.182b
sections.original: Salve , Chrysale .
sections.translation: Hail , Chrysalus , to you !

sections.label: Section 2.2.183
sections.original: Compendi verba multa iam faciam tibi .
sections.translation: I will at present compress many speeches for you in a small space . You are glad that I am come ; I give you credit for it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.185
sections.original: hospitium et cenam pollicere , ut convenit
sections.translation: You promise me lodging and an entertainment coming from my journey , as it is befitting , and I agree to come . I bring you the sincere greetings of your friend . Would you ask me where he is ? He ’ s come .

sections.label: Section 2.2.188b
sections.original: Nempe recté valet ?
sections.translation: Is he alive and well ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.189
sections.original: Istuc volebam ego ex te percontarier .
sections.translation: That I was wishful to enquire of yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.190
sections.original: Qui scire possum ?
sections.translation: How can I know ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.190b
sections.original: Nullus plus .
sections.translation: No one better .

sections.label: Section 2.2.190c
sections.original: Quemnam ad modum ?
sections.translation: Why , in what way ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.191
sections.original: Quia si illa inventa est , quam ille amat , recte valet ;
sections.translation: Because if she has been found whom he so loves , he is alive and well . If she is not found , he is not well , and is like to die . His mistress is the very life of a lover . If she is away , he is non-existent ; if she is with him , his property is non-existent , and himself worthless and wretched .

sections.label: Section 2.2.195
sections.original: sed tu quid factitasti mandatis super ?
sections.translation: But what have you done in respect of his commission ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.196
sections.original: Egon ut , quod ab illoc attigisset nuntius ,
sections.translation: And ought I not to have his request complied with against his arrival , which his messenger brought to me from him ? I ’ d sooner be dwelling in the realms of Acheron than not .

sections.label: Section 2.2.199_200
sections.original: Eho , an ínvenisti Bacchidem ?
sections.translation: How now , have you found out this Bacchis ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.199_200b
sections.original: Samiam quidem .
sections.translation: Yes , and a Samian one too .

sections.label: Section 2.2.201
sections.original: Vide quaeso , ne quis tractet illam indiligens ;
sections.translation: Prithee , do take care that no one handles her carelessly : you know how soon a Samian vessel [*] is wont to break .

sections.label: Section 2.2.203
sections.original: Iamne ut soles ?
sections.translation: What now , your old habit ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.203b
sections.original: Dic ubi ea nunc est , obsecro .
sections.translation: Prithee , do tell me where she now is .

sections.label: Section 2.2.204
sections.original: Hic , exeuntem me unde aspexisti modo .
sections.translation: Here , where you just now saw me coming out .

sections.label: Section 2.2.205
sections.original: Vt istúc est lepidum : proximae viciniae
sections.translation: How capital that is ! She ’ s living almost next door . Does she at all remember Mnesilochus ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.206b
sections.original: Rogas ?
sections.translation: Do you ask me that ? Aye , him alone does she esteem at the very highest value .

sections.label: Section 2.2.207b
sections.original: Papae .
sections.translation: Indeed !

sections.label: Section 2.2.208
sections.original: Immo ut eam credis ? misera amans desiderat .
sections.translation: Yes , and were you to believe her , distractedly in love — she quite longs for him .

sections.label: Section 2.2.209
sections.original: Scitum istuc .
sections.translation: That ’ s good .

sections.label: Section 2.2.209b
sections.original: Immo , Chrysale , em non tantulum
sections.translation: Yes , Chrysalus ; see , now ; not even so small

sections.label: Section 2.2.210
sections.original: umquam intermittit tempus quin eum nominet .
sections.translation: a space of time ever passes by as this [*] , out that she is uttering his name .

sections.label: Section 2.2.211
sections.original: Tanto hercle melior .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , so much the better .

sections.label: Section 2.2.211b
sections.original: Immo —
sections.translation: Yes ; but —

sections.label: Section 2.2.211c
sections.original: Immo hercle abiero
sections.translation: Yes , faith [*] , I ’ d rather be off .

sections.label: Section 2.2.212b
sections.original: Num invitus rem bene gestam audis eri ?
sections.translation: And do you so unwillingly hear how your master ’ s interests have prospered ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.213
sections.original: Non res , sed actor mihi cor odio sauciat .
sections.translation: No , not the subject [*] , but the actor offends my feelings with his tediousness . Even Epidicus , a play that I love quite as much as my own self ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.215
sections.original: nullam aeque invitus specto , sí agit Pellio .
sections.translation: were Pollio to act it [*] , no play would I see so reluctantly . But , does Bacchis seem handsome , as well , to you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.216b
sections.original: Rogas ?
sections.translation: Do you ask the question ? Had I not got a Venus , I should pronounce her a Juno .

sections.label: Section 2.2.218
sections.original: Edepol , Mnesiloche , ut hanc rem natam intellego ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , Mnesilochus , as I find these matters proceeding , there ’ s something ready for you to love ; ’ tis needful that you find something to give her .

sections.label: Section 2.2.220
sections.original: nam istic fortasse auro est opust .
sections.translation: But , perhaps you have need of gold for that other one ?
